channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah confronts Israeli troops advancing in southern Lebanon amid ongoing strikes
item.description: Hezbollah fighters fired rockets at Israeli troops near Majdal Zoun, forcing them to retreat, as Israel continued air strikes and issued evacuation warnings for 20 towns in southern Lebanon including Nabatieh.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian strike sets marine terminal ablaze in Russia Krasnodar Krai
item.description: A Ukrainian strike hit the Tamanneftegaz liquefied petroleum gas terminal in Russia southern Krasnodar region, with monitoring groups reporting fire signatures at the facility.

item.title: ⚔️ US downs Iranian drones targeting commercial ships in Strait of Hormuz as peace talks continue
item.description: The US military shot down multiple Iranian drones that were targeting commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, even as both Washington and Tehran insisted a peace deal had never been closer.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India successfully tests ballistic missile defence system with three flight trials
item.description: India demonstrated its ballistic missile defence capability with three successful flight tests, showing its ability to intercept incoming long-range nuclear missiles and hostile aircraft.

item.title: 🇭🇷 Croatia emerges as maritime gateway connecting Asia to Central Europe
item.description: Croatia is strengthening its role as the maritime hub linking China and other Asian nations with Central and Eastern Europe, positioning itself as a key trade route amid shifting global supply chains.

item.title: ⛪ Iran mined entrance to bombed nuclear site to deter uranium retrieval raid
item.description: Former nuclear official says the move raises risk Tehran could claim it cannot retrieve some of the material at Isfahan; US says it is close to signing a peace deal with Iran.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Trump cuts US military assets in Europe, Britain faces uncertainty
item.description: US President Donald Trump is reducing American military presence in Europe, creating uncertainty for Britain defense posture and NATO alliance commitments.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Over 225,000 Russian soldiers killed in Ukraine identified by media investigation
item.description: A media investigation has identified more than 225,000 Russian soldiers killed in Ukraine, providing a detailed accounting of Moscow military losses in the ongoing war.

item.title: 🏛️ EU envoys greenlight first phase of membership talks with Ukraine and Moldova
item.description: European Union ambassadors approved opening the first phase of membership negotiations with Ukraine and Moldova, advancing their bids to join the bloc despite ongoing challenges.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China confirms arrest of US citizen suspected of spying
item.description: Chinese authorities confirmed the detention of an American citizen on suspicion of espionage, further straining US-China relations amid ongoing tensions.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan PM says final text of US-Iran peace deal agreed, working on signing
item.description: Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced that the United States and Iran have agreed on the final text of a peace agreement to end their three-month conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China new loans miss forecasts as property slump curbs demand
item.description: China new lending fell short of economist expectations in May, as the ongoing property market slump continued to weigh on credit demand in the world second-largest economy.

item.title: 🇦🇿 Azerbaijan sentences prominent rights activist to eight years in prison
item.description: An Azerbaijani court sentenced a prominent human rights activist to eight years in prison, drawing international condemnation and raising concerns about political repression.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Evangelicals divided on Trump Iran war and immigration crackdown, Reuters/Ipsos poll shows
item.description: A Reuters/Ipsos poll reveals growing divisions among evangelical Christians over President Trump handling of the Iran war and immigration policies, a key voting bloc ahead of elections.

item.title: 🌍 US deports Iranian pro-democracy activist to Central African Republic, lawyer says
item.description: The United States deported an Iranian pro-democracy activist to the Central African Republic, his lawyer said, raising concerns about US immigration enforcement practices.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine drone strike kills one, sparks fire at Russian port of Temryuk
item.description: A Ukrainian drone strike killed one person and sparked a fire at the Russian port of Temryuk in the Krasnodar region, the regional governor said, amid ongoing attacks on Russian infrastructure.

item.title: ⚔️ Iran peace deal looms while new military action flares near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States and Iran signaled that an agreement to end their three-month war was close, though new military action continued near the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump says US military carried out strike on Tren de Aragua leader
item.description: President Donald Trump announced that the US military conducted a strike killing a leader of the Tren de Aragua cartel and US-designated terrorist organization.

item.title: 🇧🇷 Brazil refiners fueled scheme linked to US-designated terrorist group
item.description: Brazilian refiners were involved in a fuel scheme connected to a US-designated terrorist group, a source revealed, raising concerns about illicit financing networks in Latin America.

item.title: 🏛️ US ordered to reinstate references to slavery on monuments, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge ordered the US government to restore references to slavery on monuments, memorials and plaques in national parks and public spaces after they were removed under Trump administration policies.

item.title: 🏛️ Kennedy Center begins removing Trump name after court-ordered deadline
item.description: Workers began removing President Donald Trump name from the Kennedy Center facade after a court-ordered deadline, marking a significant development in the ongoing dispute over the arts institution leadership.

channel.item: 💰 EU faces backlash over Kaja Kallas foreign policy approach https://www.msn.com/en-in/politics/government/backlash-against-kallas-reveals-hard-truths-of-eu-foreign-policy/ar-AA25wGH7?ocid=BingNewsVerp <European Union foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas is facing criticism over her approach to EU foreign policy, revealing hard truths about the bloc ability to project unified positions on global affairs. Fri, 13 Jun 2026 00:00:00 +0300 Europe MSN

item.title: 🇫🇷 Modi in Nice as India and France seek to strengthen strategic partnership
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi is in Nice for talks with French President Emmanuel Macron, as the two nations seek to deepen their long-standing strategic partnership ahead of the G7 summit.

item.title: 🌏 PM Modi embarks on France, Slovakia visit with G7 summit and bilateral talks on agenda
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi departed for a week-long visit to France and Slovakia with a packed diplomatic itinerary including the G7 summit and strategic bilateral discussions.

item.title: 📻 BBC broadcast on Iran conflict available for streaming
item.description: BBC World Service broadcast covering the ongoing Iran conflict and regional implications available for on-demand streaming.
